title: specrcheckr feature tree
description: The canonical, hand-maintained map of what your product does. specrcheckr maps your code changes onto this tree to produce a human-readable review.

version.description: Optional schema/version marker for your own tracking.

root.description: The single top-level feature node. Everything nests under it.

id.description: Stable, unique id for this feature node. NEVER reuse or renumber — comments and approvals are tied to it. Convention: dotted path, e.g. 'auth.login'.

label.description: Short human name shown in the tree, e.g. 'Log in'.

description.description: Plain-English description of what this feature does TODAY. specrcheckr shows a short summary of this in the main panel and the full text under Evidence. When behavior changes, you edit this text and the tool shows the before/after.

status.description: Where this feature stands. Drives status badges and some review flags.

security.description: Set true if this feature touches auth, permissions, data access, or anything security-sensitive. Raises an 'Access / security' review flag so changes get extra scrutiny.

source.description: Implementation file paths that make up this feature. specrcheckr uses these to map a code change to this node. Keep them accurate — this mapping is how the tool knows which feature your diff touched.

tests.description: Test file paths covering this feature. A changed feature with no referenced tests raises an 'Evidence gap' flag.

docs.description: Doc paths (specs, runbooks) relevant to this feature, shown as evidence links.
